heading: Retirement benefits
label: 4009 Retirement benefits

Special Annuity for Certain Foreign Service Officers, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–105, title IV, § 41191 Stat. 855Pub. L. 95–426, title IV, § 412(b)92 Stat. 981Aug. 17, 1977Oct. 7, 1978“(a)Subject to the conditions established in subsection (b), any Foreign Service officer—“(1)who was retired under section 633(a)(1) of the Foreign Service Act of 1946 [former , see ] before the date of enactment of this section, [];section 1003(a)(1) of this titlesection 4007(a) of this titleAug. 17, 1977“(2)who was not in class 1, 2, or 3 at the time of retirement;“(3)who was 40 years of age or older at the time of retirement; and“(4)who had at least 20 years of service, exclusive of credit for unused sick leave, creditable for purposes of section 821 of such Act [former , see ] at the time of retirement;section 1076 of this titlesection 4046 of this titleshall be entitled to receive retirement benefits in accordance with the provisions of such section 821 [former , see ] in lieu of any retirement benefits which the officer may be entitled to elect under section 634(b)(2) of such Act [former , see subsec. (b)(2) of this section]. Such retirement benefits shall be paid from the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund and shall be effective on the date the officer reaches age 50, the date of enactment of this section [], or , whichever date is latest.section 1076 of this titlesection 4046 of this titlesection 1004(b)(2) of this titleAug. 17, 1977October 1, 1977“(b)Retirement benefits may not be paid under this section unless (1) any refund of contributions paid to the officer under section 634(b)(2) of the Foreign Service Act of 1946 [former , see subsec. (b)(2) of this section] is repaid to the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund, with interest, in accordance with sections 811(d) and (f) of such Act [former section 1071(d) and (f) of this title, see section 4045(d) and (f) of this title]; and (2) the service forming the basis for such retirement benefits is not used as the basis for any other retirement benefits under any retirement system.section 1004(b)(2) of this title“(c)In the event that an officer who is entitled to retirement benefits under this section dies before reaching the age of fifty, but after the date of enactment of this section [], his or her death shall be considered a death in service within the meaning of section 832 of the Foreign Service Act of 1946 [former , see ], except that no survivor’s annuity (other than a survivor’s annuity which would be payable under the first complete sentence in section 634(b)(2) of such Act [former , see subsec. (b)(2) of this section] but for the enactment of this section) shall become effective before .Aug. 17, 1977October 1, 1977section 1082 of this titlesection 4049 of this titlesection 1004(b)(2) of this title“(d)An officer entitled to retirement benefits under this section may make the election described in section 821(b) or (f), as appropriate, of the Foreign Service Act of 1946 [former section 1076(b) or (f) of this title, see section 4046(b) and (f) of this title] at any time before reaching the age of fifty or before the end of the sixty-day period beginning on the date of enactment of this section [], whichever is later.”Aug. 17, 1977

children.text: who is retired under ; orsection 4007(c)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: after becoming eligible for voluntary retirement under or any other applicable provision of chapter 84 of title 5, orsection 4051 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: from the Senior Foreign Service or while assigned to class 1 in the Foreign Service Schedule,

children.heading: Computation of amount; refund; death of member
children.label: b Computation of amount; refund; death of member

children.text: one-twelfth of a year’s salary at his or her then current salary rate for each year of service and proportionately for a fraction of a year, but not exceeding a total of one year’s salary at his or her then current salary rate, payable without interest from the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund in 3 equal installments, such installments to be paid on January 1 of each of the first 3 calendar years beginning after the retirement of the member (except that in special cases, the Secretary of State may accelerate or combine such installments); and

children.label: A
children.text: for those participants in the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System, a refund as provided in of the contributions made by the member to the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability Fund, except that in lieu of such refund a member who has at least 5 years of service credit toward retirement under the Foreign Service Retirement and Disability System (excluding military and naval service) may elect to receive an annuity, computed under , commencing at age 60; and (B) for those participants in the Foreign Service Pension System, benefits as provided in .section 4055 of this titlesection 4046 of this titlesection 4071 of this title
